title: Malaga, 22-25 Apr 2026
subtitle: Four days exploring Andalusia's coastal gem — sun-drenched tapas bars, sunset catamarans, and a cliff-edge day trip to Ronda.

images.bannerAlt: Malaga harbour and cathedral from the sea at golden hour

day1.alt: Malaga Cathedral rising above the old town rooftops at golden hour

day2.alt: Ronda's Puente Nuevo bridge spanning the dramatic El Tajo gorge
White houses perched on the cliff edge overlooking the gorge in Ronda

day3.alt: Malaga port and marina at sunset with mountains in the background
Playa de la Malagueta beach with palm trees and clear Mediterranean water

day4.alt: La Farola lighthouse at Malaga port with palm trees

translation.commonPhrases: Hola (Hello) • Gracias (Thank you) • ¿Cuánto cuesta? (How much?) • La cuenta, por favor (The bill, please)

accommodation.name: Coeo Frailes Design Apartments
accommodation.instructions: Check-in at Coeo Pod Hostel, Calle Beatas 20 (short walk). Smart-lock keyless entry at the apartment.

airportTransfer.name: Airport Express Bus (Line A)
airportTransfer.details: Every 20 mins from AGP to city centre (Paseo del Parque). ~€3, takes 20-25 mins. Or train (Line C1) from airport station to Malaga Centro-Alameda, ~12 mins, ~€1.80.
airportTransfer.note: The Express Bus A drops you closest to the old town. Taxi to centre is around €15-20.

gettingAround.modes: Walking primarily. Everything in the centre is within 15-20 mins on foot.
gettingAround.appNote: For day trips, buses depart from Estación de Autobuses (Paseo de los Tilos). Trains from María Zambrano station.

weather.summary: Late April is warm and sunny. Expect 20-24°C daytime, dropping to 13-15°C after dark. Sea temperature around 17°C — refreshing but swimmable for the bold. Occasional light rain possible but unlikely. Sunset around 21:00.

packing: Light layers, sunglasses, sun cream, comfortable walking shoes for cobblestones, swimwear, a light jacket for evenings on the water, camera for Ronda's gorge.
farewell: Hope you and Claire had an amazing time in Malaga!
footer: Have an incredible trip to Malaga!

Romancero.name: Uvedoble Taberna
Romancero.why: Modern Malagueno tapas near the Roman Theatre. Three portion sizes for flexible sharing.
Romancero.dishes: Ajoblanco, aubergine with cane honey, Ibérico pork.

La Cosmo.name: Beluga
La Cosmo.why: Mediterranean seafood and exceptional rice dishes in a gorgeous square with orange trees.
La Cosmo.dishes: Shell-free rice with monkfish, grilled sea bass.

La Barra de Zapata.name: Meson Mariano
La Barra de Zapata.why: Family-run since 1988, seasonal Andalusian home cooking. Locals' favourite.
La Barra de Zapata.dishes: Fried fish, pork cheeks, seasonal market dishes.

El Tapeo de Cervantes.name: Kortxo
El Tapeo de Cervantes.why: Creative fusion tapas with excellent tasting menu value.
El Tapeo de Cervantes.dishes: Seasonal tasting plates, creative sharing dishes.

Chester & Punk.name: The Pharmacy Speakeasy
Chester & Punk.why: Prohibition-era speakeasy with low lighting, jazz, and herbal-infused cocktails. Walk-in only.
Chester & Punk.drinks: House favourite: Krypton. Classic and creative options.

Mañana Cocktail Bar.name: Puerta Oscura
Mañana Cocktail Bar.why: Victorian time-capsule bar from 1991. Period-dressed waiters, armchairs, classical music. Unique atmosphere.
Mañana Cocktail Bar.drinks: Legendary gin and tonics, classic cocktails.

The Pharmacy Speakeasy.name: Baires Coffee & Drinks
The Pharmacy Speakeasy.why: Argentinian-run, classy without pretension. Terrace and indoor seating.
The Pharmacy Speakeasy.drinks: Piña colada in pineapple glass, classic cocktails.

Meson Iberico.name: Solera Soho
Meson Iberico.why: National Gastronomy Awards 2025 Gold Medal winner. Revived Malaga classics with a blind tasting menu.
Meson Iberico.dishes: Blind tasting menu, modern Malaga cuisine.

activities.name: Wander Plaza de la Merced and old town streets

sections.title: Airport → Calle Frailes 9-11
sections.content: Route: **Express Bus A** from airport to **Paseo del Parque** (20 mins, ~€3) then 10 min walk through the old town to the apartment. Alternative: **C1 train** to María Zambrano then walk 15 mins. Check in at **Coeo Pod Hostel on Calle Beatas 20** first to collect your smart-lock access.
sections.wet: Same route — everything is covered arcades and narrow streets.

sections.title: Late afternoon wander
sections.content: Ease into the trip with a stroll from your door. **Plaza de la Merced** is 1 minute away — Picasso's birthplace square, great for people-watching. Then drift south through the narrow streets towards **Calle Larios** (the main pedestrian boulevard) and **Plaza de la Constitución**. The old town is gorgeous in the golden hour light — all warm stone, iron balconies, and jasmine.

sections.heading: Dinner at Romancero
sections.description: Charming spot tucked into the historic Pasaje Chinitas alley. Warm host, traditional Andalusian tapas done with care. The octopus leg is exceptional, and the gambas al ajillo are properly garlicky. Closed Tuesdays — you're here on Wednesday so you're fine.
sections.prices: Tapas and mains approximately €20-35 per person.

sections.heading: Cocktails at Chester & Punk
sections.description: Your favourite from last time — still thriving. Two-sided concept: the Chester side has Chesterfield sofas and old-school elegance; the Punk side has neon, graffiti, and louder energy. Tiki-style glasses (pandas, skulls, octopuses). Try The Walking Dead (limited to two per person). Awarded a star by Top Cocktail Bars guide.

activities.name: Puente Nuevo and El Tajo gorge
Dinner at El Tapeo de Cervantes

sections.title: Early start for Ronda
sections.content: This is the one day to set an alarm. **Train departs María Zambrano at 09:07**, changes at Antequera-Santa Ana, arrives Ronda ~11:15. Fare from **€13.80 one way** — book on Renfe. The mountain scenery on the approach is stunning. Allow 15 mins walk from your apartment to the station.
sections.note: Book train tickets in advance on renfe.com — they do sell out.

sections.title: Puente Nuevo & El Tajo Gorge
sections.content: One of the most dramatic sights in all of Spain. The bridge spans a 120m deep gorge — free to walk across. Best photo spots: **Mirador Puente Nuevo** (the classic angle), **Arco del Cristo viewpoint** (from below), and **Mirador de Aldehuela**. The **Camino del Desfiladero del Tajo** (€5) takes you along the cliff face to the base of the bridge — outstanding for photography.

sections.title: Old Moorish Quarter
sections.content: After the bridge, wander **La Ciudad** — the old Moorish quarter with cobblestone streets and whitewashed houses. **Jardines de Cuenca** (rose gardens with gorge views) are lovely. The **Plaza de Toros** is one of Spain's oldest bullrings and architecturally striking even if bullfighting isn't your thing.

sections.title: Lunch in Ronda
sections.content: You'll find plenty of options around Plaza España and the old town. Look for places with gorge views — locals recommend spots on **Calle Virgen de la Paz** or near the Alameda del Tajo park. Try **rabo de toro** (oxtail stew) — Ronda's signature dish.
**17:00** Return train to Malaga. You'll be back by ~19:15 with time to freshen up before dinner.

sections.heading: Dinner at El Tapeo de Cervantes
sections.description: Creative tapas institution with a strong local following. Intimate but properly seated — no perching at a bar. The tuna ceviche with avocado and crispy plantain is a standout, and the wine list is excellent. **Book ahead** — call +34 952 609 458. Closed Mondays (you're here Thursday).

sections.heading: Cocktails at The Pharmacy Speakeasy
sections.description: Prohibition-era speakeasy with low lighting, jazz, and vintage decor. Award-winning bartender. House favourite: Krypton. Complimentary water and snacks with your cocktails. Walk-in only — no reservations. Mon-Thu open 7pm-2am.

locations.name: Mañana Cocktail Bar
locations.address: Calle San Juan de Letran 7, Malaga

activities.name: Morning at Playa de la Malagueta
Lunch at La Cosmo (Michelin Bib Gourmand)
Explore Alcazaba and Roman Theatre area
Sunset catamaran cruise from Muelle Uno

sections.title: Morning at the beach
sections.content: **11:00** Walk 15 mins from the apartment to **Playa de la Malagueta** — the city's main beach. It's sandy, clean, and backed by palm-lined promenades. In late April expect ~24°C air and ~17°C sea — refreshing but swimmable if you're brave. There are chiringuitos (beach bars) for a cold drink.
sections.wet: Skip the beach and head to the Centre Pompidou Malaga (the colourful cube on the port) — quick visit of the temporary exhibitions.

sections.heading: Lunch at La Cosmo
sections.description: Michelin Bib Gourmand restaurant by chef Dani Carnero (who also runs Michelin-starred Kaleja). Modern Andalusian seafood with an open kitchen. The interior won an iF Design Award — sleek, white-toned, contemporary. Try the hake salad (the chef's mother's recipe, singled out by Michelin inspectors). Sit at the bar facing the kitchen for the best experience. **Closed Sundays** — you're here Friday.

sections.title: Afternoon exploring
sections.content: **15:30** Walk off lunch through the streets around the **Cathedral** and up to the **Alcazaba** area. You've been here before but it's worth revisiting the views from the terraces in the afternoon light. The **Roman Theatre** below is free to view. Wander the streets of the old town — Calle San Agustín, Calle Granada — looking for street art and photography angles.
sections.wet: CAC Malaga (Centre for Contemporary Art) — free entry, usually has interesting exhibitions in the converted wholesale market building.

sections.title: Sunset Catamaran Cruise
items: **Mundo Marino** sunset catamaran — 1.5 hours from Muelle Uno
Departs late afternoon (check exact time for April 24th)
**€18 per person** — includes a glass of cava
Live music on Fridays! You're here on Friday 🎵
Bar on board (sangrias ~€5). Net seating area €6 extra

sections.heading: Tapas at Meson Iberico
sections.description: The Soho district standout — 20+ years running, packed with locals. Proper sit-down tapas, not standing at a bar. Over 3,500 Google reviews at 4.7 stars. Everything is good but the Ibérico cuts are the star.

sections.heading: Late drinks at Mañana
sections.address: Calle San Juan de Letran 7, Malaga
sections.description: Small, cozy, charming — feels like a local secret. Run by a Latvian owner who came for Erasmus and stayed. 80+ cocktails on the menu. The raspberry daiquiri is legendary. Warm and unpretentious with great music. Can get busy (which adds atmosphere). More intimate than Chester & Punk — nice contrast.
sections.prices: Cocktails reasonable — lower end of Malaga cocktail bar prices.

activities.name: Late brunch at La Barra de Zapata
Final wander through Soho street art

sections.title: Last morning in Malaga
sections.content: **10:30** Check out of Coeo Frailes (checkout by 11:00). They offer luggage storage at the Pod Hostel on Calle Beatas — drop your bags there.

**11:00** If you haven't revisited **Atarazanas Market** yet, swing through — the Moorish gate entrance is beautiful, and the stalls inside are great for picking up last-minute olives, spices, or dried fruit to take home. Best on weekday mornings.

sections.heading: Brunch at La Barra de Zapata
sections.description: Family-owned, central, atmospheric — the best El Pimpi alternative without the tourist crowds. Cathedral views, highly rated by locals. A perfect final meal.
sections.prices: Tapas and mains approximately €20-35 per person.

sections.content: **13:30** Final stroll through **Soho** if time allows — check for any new murals since your last visit. Then collect bags from Calle Beatas and head to the airport via **Express Bus A** from Paseo del Parque or grab a taxi (~€15-20, 15 mins).

sections.title: If you have extra time
sections.content:
**Rooftop drink**: AC Hotel Malaga Palacio (ATICO Bar) on the 15th floor — the highest rooftop bar in Malaga with 360° views over the city, harbour, and Mediterranean. Cocktails €8-15. Perfect if your flight is later.

or **Flamenco**: If you haven't caught a show, **Peña Juan Breva** on Calle Beatas 21 (right where your luggage is!) has the world's oldest flamenco pena with a museum and genuine performances.
